trajectory_summary: Richard is exceptionally self-qualified for this call type — existing solar owner, outright ownership, 770 credit, clear add-on need, and proactively helpful throughout. Parker moved the call to a booked appointment efficiently, but the booking is structurally fragile: the orphaned_flow 'system working' check was skipped (a hard DQ item), the bill was not collected, and the appointment was set conditionally on bill receipt. The call's shape is mixed — good momentum on scheduling, real gaps on qualification completeness.
outcome_dispute: The call ends with a Wednesday 11am appointment booked, but the booking is conditional — Parker explicitly states 'we do just need to look at your bill to finalize that' at 04:43, and Richard's parting line is 'If I can't get it, I'll call you back.' This is a tentative appointment, not a confirmed sit. If this is a paid lead (lead type unconfirmed — manager should verify), the bill is required before the sit can be counted, making the booking potentially uncountable for compensation purposes until the bill arrives.

primary_coaching_focus.topic: Orphaned-flow protocol completion — missing hard DQ check
primary_coaching_focus.quote: I have solar already. I already sent in my quest. I have a new a new addition I put on. I like to have that connected.
primary_coaching_focus.why: The moment Richard disclosed existing solar, the call became an orphaned_flow scenario. Parker completed two of the four orphaned_flow steps (company and ownership) but skipped the 'Is your system working?' check — a hard DQ item. The closer is walking into Wednesday's appointment without knowing whether Richard's existing system is functional. If it isn't, the appointment disqualifies at proposal and Parker doesn't get paid. This is the highest-leverage coaching gap on this call because it's both a protocol failure and a compensation risk.

key_moments.quote: I have solar already. I already sent in my quest. I have a new a new addition I put on. I like to have that connected.
key_moments.stewart_read: This line triggers the orphaned_flow protocol. Parker correctly asks about company and ownership (00:54–01:07), but never asks 'Is your system working?' — the hard DQ check in the schema. A non-working existing system is a hard disqualifier. Parker moved to credit qualification without completing this check.

key_moments.quote: The last one the last one was 80 some dollars. One before that was $2.20 something, and the one for that was $2.30 something. Why it went down to 80 something, I don't know. But Yeah.
key_moments.stewart_read: Richard volunteers three months of bill history unprompted, including a puzzling drop he can't explain. This is a live curiosity hook — Richard is already asking himself why his bill behaved oddly. Parker acknowledges the dollar figures are 'a little helpful' but immediately pivots to chasing kWh data, walking past the emotional opening entirely. The unexplained bill drop is exactly the kind of anchor the schema's bill_anchoring section describes: capture + flip into alignment-against-utility. Parker captured and dropped.

key_moments.quote: I don't want anything. And I have electric heat and air conditioning.
key_moments.stewart_read: Richard discloses electric heat and air conditioning — a high-load profile that makes the add-on system more valuable and the design more complex. Parker does not acknowledge this disclosure at all. Electric heat is a load signal that should inform system sizing and strengthen the case for the add-on. Parker was mid-bill-chase and missed it entirely.

key_moments.quote: Alright. They'll they'll email you the design as well so that you can see what we're talking about and everything like that.
key_moments.stewart_read: Parker states the design will be emailed to Richard before the appointment. This is ambiguous — it may describe the appointment mechanics (closer emails design during the call) or it may set an expectation that Richard will receive a design before the Wednesday call. If Richard interprets this as a pre-meeting design delivery, it creates a misaligned expectation the closer will have to manage. The schema's send_proposal_before_meeting rebuttal is triggered by customer requests; this was unprompted by Richard. The framing is a coaching gap in appointment-mechanics communication, not a hard-rule violation.
